titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Arthur Munro to Mabel FitzGerald, 24 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Arthur Munro

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is from C.A. Munro, governor of Mountjoy prison, to Mabel FitzGerald (née Washington McConnell) (1884-1958), wife of politician and intellectual Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947). This letter was written while Desmond FitzGerald was serving a six month sentence in Mountjoy prison having been convicted under the new Defence of the Realm legislation for making a seditious speech. He later served in the GPO during the Easter Rising, being placed in charge of the garrison's food supply. He initially escaped arrest after the Rising but was soon rounded up and imprisoned in Mountjoy, Dartmoor, Maidstone, Lewes and Portland. Munro writes about a proposed visit by Mabel to her husband that will be of 'strictly business nature'.

correspAction.placeName: 2 Loretto Villas, Bray, County Wicklow, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
H.M. Prison,
Mountjoy. 24th Nov. 15 Dear Madam With reference to your
letter of 21st last. I am to inform
you that when I have looked through
the M.S.S. left by you I will notify
you as to when you can see your
husband. As this visit is of a
strictly business nature I repeat
that it will not be permissible
for you to bring your little son on the
occasion of the interview. Yours faithfully C.T. Munro Mrs. Fitzgerald
2 Loretto Villas
Bray
